title: phlox speciosa subsp. nitida
intro: Phlox speciosa subsp. nitida is a subspecies of the species Phlox speciosa, a flowering plant native to North America, known for its fragrant flowers and medicinal properties. The plant has been traditionally used for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties, making it useful in treating various health issues. The medicinal properties of Phlox speciosa subsp. nitida include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and pneumonia. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal benefits and promote overall health and wellness.
